ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the performance data */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the performance data */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the performance data */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Exact timestamp when the performance data was collected */,
  "average_rtt" REAL /* Average Round Trip Time (RTT) in milliseconds */,
  "average_rtt_n" INTEGER /* Number of samples used to calculate the average RTT */,
  "download_throughput" REAL /* Average download throughput in Mbps */,
  "download_throughput_n" INTEGER /* Number of samples used to calculate the average download throughput */,
  "minimum_rtt" REAL /* Minimum Round Trip Time (RTT) in milliseconds */,
  "minimum_rtt_n" INTEGER /* Number of samples used to calculate the minimum RTT */,
  "packet_retransmit_rate" REAL /* Packet retransmission rate as a percentage */,
  "packet_retransmit_rate_n" INTEGER /* Number of samples used to calculate the packet retransmission rate */,
  "upload_throughput" REAL /* Average upload throughput in Mbps */,
  "upload_throughput_n" INTEGER /* Number of samples used to calculate the average upload throughput */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network node where the data was collected */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the node is located */,
  "status" TEXT /* Overall status of the network node (e.g., normal, warning, critical) */,
  "jitter" REAL /* Jitter (variation in RTT) in milliseconds */,
  "packet_loss_rate" REAL /* Packet loss rate as a percentage */,
  "data_volume" REAL /* Total data volume handled by the node in GB */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_performance_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_performance_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES network_nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network node */,
  "node_name" TEXT /* Name of the network node */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the network node */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network node */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the node is located */,
  "node_type" TEXT /* Type of the network node (e.g., data center, edge node, core router) */,
  "uptime" INTEGER /* Uptime of the node in hours */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "hardware_version" TEXT /* Version of the hardware */,
  "software_version" TEXT /* Version of the software */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person responsible for the node */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email of the contact person */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact person */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_nodes_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network engineer, IT admin, manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance record being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the data */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Exact time when the data was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES network_performance ("performance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)
